metadata.title: Retrieval Drift
metadata.subtitle: Grounding failure packet
metadata.description: A knowledge packet focused on grounding drift: the answer is plausible, but citations and figures start to pull apart.
watchFor: The answer should stay readable while low-confidence evidence and contradictions compete around it.
Citation-linked evidence should cluster, but the weakest citation should visibly destabilize its neighborhood.
The same packet should remain understandable in plain view before field styling is layered on top.

guideSteps.label: Main answer
guideSteps.description: The answer is fluent but only partially grounded.

guideSteps.label: Weak citation
guideSteps.description: This citation is intentionally low confidence and should feel less stable.

guideSteps.label: Grounding risk
guideSteps.description: The contradiction should stay close enough to explain the weakness without replacing the answer.

tasks.title: Trace the grounded path
tasks.prompt: Follow the strongest answer/evidence/citation chain and see whether it remains co-visible.

tasks.title: Find the weak link
tasks.prompt: Spot the weakest citation region without losing the supporting answer context.

claim.title: Grounded Answers Depend on Citation Adjacency
claim.statement: When support chains drift apart, an answer may remain fluent while its actual grounding becomes difficult to inspect.

answerBlocks.text: Strong evidence should stay near the claim, but weak citations should not disappear behind a rigid reading stack.

evidence.text: Retrieval traces align with the claim when citation neighborhoods stay compact.
A weaker citation leaves the answer partially unsupported even if the prose still reads smoothly.
A plain renderer still needs semantic ordering so evidence does not collapse into a flat list.

contradictions.text: The weakest citation is too far from the claim to support fast audit.
